title.zh: 卷二十 銚期王霸祭遵列傳
title.en: Volume 20: Biographies of Tiao Qi; Wang Ba; Zhai Zun
title.raw: 卷二十 銚期王霸祭遵列傳 Volume 20: Biographies of Tiao Qi; Wang Ba; Zhai Zun

translations.literal: 731 page.
translations.idiomatic: Source edition, page 731.

sentences.zh: 銚期字次況，潁川郟人也。

translations.literal: Tiao Qi styled Cikuang, was a man of Jia in Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Tiao Qi, courtesy name Cikuang, was a native of Jia in Yingchuan commandery.

sentences.zh: 長八尺二寸，容貌絕異，矜嚴有威。

translations.literal: Height eight chi two cun, appearance countenance absolutely unusual, stern self-contained had awe.
translations.idiomatic: He stood eight chi and two cun tall, with striking looks and a severe, awe-inspiring bearing.

sentences.zh: 父猛，為桂陽太守，卒，期服喪三年，鄉里稱之。

translations.literal: Father Meng, was Guiyang governor, died, Qi observed mourning three years, village praised him.
translations.idiomatic: His father Tiao Meng had been the governor of Guiyang; when he died, Qi mourned three full years, and the neighbors praised him.

sentences.zh: 光武略地潁川，聞期誌義，召署賊曹掾[1]，從徇薊[2]。

translations.literal: Guangwu seized territory Yingchuan, heard Qi's resolve and righteousness, summoned appointed bandit bureau clerk [1], followed campaign Ji [2].
translations.idiomatic: While Guangwu was taking Yingchuan he heard of Qi's steadfast loyalty, summoned him as clerk of the bandit bureau [1], and took him on the expedition to Ji [2].

sentences.zh: 時，王郎檄書到薊，薊中起兵應郎。

translations.literal: At that time, Wang Lang's proclamation arrived Ji, Ji within raised troops responded Lang.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Lang's summons reached Ji, and the city rose in arms for him.

sentences.zh: 光武趨駕出，百姓聚觀，喧呼滿道，遮路不得行，期騎馬奮戟，瞋目大呼左右曰「□」[3]，眾皆披靡[4]。

translations.literal: Guangwu hurried carriage out, hundred surnames gathered watched, clamor shout filled road, blocked road not obtain go, Qi rode horse brandished halberd, glared eyes great shouted to left right saying "(manuscript lacuna)" [3], masses all scattered [4].
translations.idiomatic: As Guangwu spurred his carriage out, the crowd mobbed the street and blocked the way. Qi rode forward, whirling his halberd, glared, and roared "Clear the road" [3] until the mob broke and scattered [4].

sentences.zh: 及至城門，門已閉，攻之得出。

translations.literal: When reached city gate, gate already closed, attacked it obtained exit.
translations.idiomatic: They reached the city gate to find it shut; they forced it and got out.

sentences.zh: 行至信都，以期為裨將，與傅寬、呂晏俱屬鄧禹。

translations.literal: Marched reached Xindu, made Qi lieutenant general, with Fu Kuan, Lü Yan together subordinate to Deng Yu.
translations.idiomatic: At Xindu he was made a lieutenant general under Deng Yu, alongside Fu Kuan and Lü Yan.

sentences.zh: 徇傍縣，又發房子兵。

translations.literal: Campaigned neighboring counties, again raised Fangzi troops.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned the neighboring counties and also levied troops from Fangzi.

sentences.zh: 禹以期為能，獨拜偏將軍，授兵二千人，寬、晏各數百人。

translations.literal: Yu because Qi capable, alone appointed lieutenant general, gave troops two thousand men, Kuan, Yan each several hundred men.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu, judging Qi especially able, commissioned him alone as lieutenant general with two thousand men, while Fu Kuan and Lü Yan received only a few hundred each.

sentences.zh: 還言其狀，光武甚善之。

translations.literal: Returned spoke the situation, Guangwu greatly approved him.
translations.idiomatic: On his return he reported how things stood, and Guangwu was much pleased.

sentences.zh: 使期別徇真定宋子，攻拔樂陽[5]、槁[6]、肥累[7]。

translations.literal: Ordered Qi separately campaign Zhending Songzi, attacked and took Leyang [5], Gao [6], Feilei [7].
translations.idiomatic: He was sent to campaign separately in Zhending and Songzi, capturing Leyang [5], Gao [6], and Feilei [7].
Source edition, pages 732-741.

sentences.zh: 從擊王郎將兒宏[8]、劉奉於鉅鹿下，期先登陷陳，手殺五十餘人，被創中額，攝*

translations.literal: Followed struck Wang Lang's generals Er Hong [8], Liu Feng below Julu, Qi first ascended broke array, hand killed more than fifty men, received wound middle forehead, *
translations.idiomatic: Under Julu he joined the attack on Wang Lang's generals Er Hong [8] and Liu Feng: Qi was first on the wall, killed more than fifty men with his own hand, and took a blow to the forehead—

translations.literal: (head-wrap variant)
translations.idiomatic: (textual note: head-wrap variant)

sentences.zh: **[幘]*復戰[9]，遂大破之。

translations.literal: **[headband]* fought again [9], thereupon greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: —bound up his wound with a headcloth and fought on [9], and they routed the enemy.

sentences.zh: 王郎滅，拜期虎牙大將軍。

translations.literal: Wang Lang extinguished, appointed Qi tiger-fang great general.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Lang fell, Qi was named tiger-fang grand general.

sentences.zh: 乃因閒說光武曰：「河北之地，界接邊塞，人習兵戰，號為精勇。

translations.literal: Then by opportunity told Guangwu saying: "Hebei land, border joins frontier passes, people practice arms war, titled crack brave.
translations.idiomatic: He found a private moment to say to Guangwu, "Hebei borders the frontier; its people are inured to war and famed as fierce fighters.

sentences.zh: 今更始失政，大統危殆，海內無所歸往。

translations.literal: Now Gengshi loses government, great line endangered, within seas nowhere to turn.
translations.idiomatic: The Gengshi court has lost its grip; the imperial line totters, and the realm has nowhere to look for leadership.

sentences.zh: 明公據河山之固，擁精銳之觿，以順萬人思漢之心，則天下誰敢不從？

translations.literal: Illustrious lord occupies river mountains' firmness, holds crack sharp masses, by comply ten thousand persons think Han heart, then under Heaven who dares not follow?
translations.idiomatic: You hold the natural fortress of the Yellow River and Taihang and command picked troops; if you answer the people's longing for Han, who under Heaven would dare refuse you?

sentences.zh: 」光武笑曰：「卿欲遂前□邪？

translations.literal: " Guangwu laughed saying: "You desire carry through earlier counsel evil?
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu laughed. "You want to press that old counsel of yours, do you?"

sentences.zh: 」[10]時銅馬數十萬觿入清陽、博平[11]，期與諸將迎擊之，連戰不利，期乃更背水而戰，所殺傷甚多。

translations.literal: " [10] At that time Bronze Horse several ten thousand masses entered Qingyang, Boping [11], Qi with various generals met struck them, continuous battles not favorable, Qi then changed back water and battled, kill wound very many.
translations.idiomatic: " [10] Just then several tens of thousands of Bronze Horse troops poured into Qingyang and Boping [11]; Qi and the other generals met them in a run of reverses, so Qi drew his line with its back to the water and fought again, inflicting heavy casualties.

sentences.zh: 會光武救至，遂大破之，追至館陶，皆降之。

translations.literal: It happened Guangwu relief arrived, thereupon greatly defeated them, pursued to Guantao, all surrendered them.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu's reinforcements arrived; they broke the enemy and chased them to Guantao, where all surrendered.

sentences.zh: 從擊青犢、赤眉於射犬，賊襲期輜重，期還擊之，手殺傷數十人，身被三創，而戰方力[12]，遂破走之。

translations.literal: Followed struck Green Calf, Red Eyebrows at Shequan, bandits raided Qi's baggage train, Qi returned struck them, hand killed wounded several tens, body received three wounds, yet battle still forceful [12], thereupon defeated routed them.
translations.idiomatic: He joined the fight against the Green Calf and Red Eyebrows at Shequan; when the rebels hit his baggage train he turned back, killed or wounded dozens with his own hand though thrice wounded, and still fought with full force [12] until the enemy broke and fled.

sentences.zh: 光武即位，封安成侯，[一]食邑五千戶。

translations.literal: Guangwu ascended throne, enfeoffed Anacheng marquis, [one] food fief five thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu took the throne, Qi was enfeoffed as marquis of Ancheng [one] with a fief of five thousand households.

sentences.zh: 時檀鄉、五樓賊入繁陽、內黃，[二] 又魏郡大姓數反覆，而更始將卓京[三]謀欲相率反鄴城。

translations.literal: At that time Tanxiang, Wulou bandits entered Fanyang, Neihuang, [two] again Wei commandery great clans several times reversed, yet Gengshi general Zhuo Jing [three] plotted desire mutually lead rebel Ye city.
translations.idiomatic: Tanxiang and Wulou raiders had entered Fanyang and Neihuang [two]; the great clans of Wei were shifting allegiance again and again, and the Gengshi general Zhuo Jing [three] plotted to lead a mutiny back into Ye.

sentences.zh: 帝以期為魏郡太守，行大將軍事。

translations.literal: Emperor made Qi Wei commandery governor, acting great general affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor named Qi governor of Wei commandery with the powers of a grand general.

sentences.zh: 期發郡兵擊卓京，破之，斬首六百餘級。

translations.literal: Qi raised commandery troops struck Zhuo Jing, defeated him, beheaded heads more than six hundred grades.
translations.idiomatic: Qi raised the commandery troops, defeated Zhuo Jing, and took more than six hundred heads.

sentences.zh: 京亡入山，追斬其將校數十人，獲京妻子。

translations.literal: Jing fled entered mountain, pursued beheaded his colonels several tens of men, captured Jing's wife children.
translations.idiomatic: Jing fled into the hills; the pursuit cut down dozens of his officers and seized his wife and children.

sentences.zh: 進擊繁陽、內黃，復斬數百級，郡界清平。

translations.literal: Advanced struck Fanyang, Neihuang, again beheaded several hundred grades, commandery border clear peaceful.
translations.idiomatic: He pushed on into Fanyang and Neihuang, took another few hundred heads, and the commandery was quiet.

sentences.zh: 督盜賊李熊，鄴中之豪，而熊弟陸謀欲反城迎檀鄉。

translations.literal: Supervisor of bandits Li Xiong, Ye within's magnate, yet Xiong's younger brother Lu plotted desire open city welcome Tanxiang.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xiong, who supervised bandit affairs, was a leading man of Ye; his younger brother Li Lu plotted to open the gates for the Tanxiang raiders.

sentences.zh: [四]或以告期，期不應，告者三四，期乃召問熊。

translations.literal: [four] Someone reported to Qi, Qi not respond, reporters three four times, Qi then summoned asked Xiong.
translations.idiomatic: [Four] Informants came to Qi, who at first made no move; when they came three or four times he summoned Li Xiong for questioning.

sentences.zh: 熊叩頭首服，願與老母俱就死。

translations.literal: Xiong knocked head confessed submission, wished with old mother together go die.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xiong kowtowed and confessed, ready to die with his aged mother.

sentences.zh: 期曰：「為吏儻不若為賊樂者，可歸與老母往就陸也。

translations.literal: Qi said: 'If as clerk perhaps not like as bandit happy, can return with old mother go join Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Qi said, 'If you think office less pleasant than brigandage, go home and take your mother to your brother Lu.'

sentences.zh: 」[五] 使吏送出城。

translations.literal: ' [five] Ordered clerk escort out city.
translations.idiomatic: [Five] He had clerks escort him beyond the walls.

sentences.zh: 熊行求得陸，將詣鄴城西門。

translations.literal: Xiong walking sought obtained Lu, led to Ye city west gate.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xiong found Li Lu and brought him to the west gate of Ye.

sentences.zh: 陸不勝愧感，自殺以謝期。

translations.literal: Lu not bear shame gratitude, self-killed to apologize Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Overcome with shame and gratitude, Li Lu killed himself to answer Qi.

sentences.zh: 期嗟歎，以禮葬之，而還熊故職。

translations.literal: Qi sighed, by rites buried him, while returned Xiong former office.
translations.idiomatic: Qi sighed, buried him with full rites, and restored Li Xiong to his old post.

sentences.zh: 於是郡中服其威信。

translations.literal: Thereupon commandery within submitted his awed trust.
translations.idiomatic: The whole commandery then deferred to his majesty and good faith.

sentences.zh: 注[一]安成，縣名，屬汝南郡，故城在今豫州汝陽縣東南也。

translations.literal: Note [one] Ancheng, county name, belonged Runan commandery, old city at today Yuzhou Ruyang county southeast.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: Ancheng was a county in Runan commandery; its old walled town lay southeast of present-day Ruyang in Yuzhou.

sentences.zh: 注[二]繁陽，縣名，故城在今相州內黃縣東北；

translations.literal: Note [two] Fanyang, county name, old city at today Xiangzhou Neihuang county northeast;
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [two]: Fanyang was a county; its old site lay northeast of present-day Neihuang in Xiangzhou;

sentences.zh: 內黃故城在西北。

translations.literal: Neihuang old city in northwest.
translations.idiomatic: the old site of Neihuang lay to the northwest.

sentences.zh: 注[三]「京」或作「原」。

translations.literal: Note [three] "Jing" sometimes written "Yuan".
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [three]: The graph "Jing" is sometimes written "Yuan".

sentences.zh: 注[四]反音翻。

translations.literal: Note [four] "fan" sound "turn".
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [four]: The gloss reads "fan" in the sense of turning or reversing.

sentences.zh: 注[五]必以在城中為吏不如為賊之樂，即任將母往就弟。

translations.literal: Note [five] must think in city as clerk not like as bandit's joy, then allow lead mother go join younger brother.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [five]: If they truly thought life as a clerk in the city less happy than banditry, he let them take their mother to the younger brother.

sentences.zh: 建武五年，行幸魏郡，以期為太中大夫。

translations.literal: Jianwu fifth year, imperial progress visited Wei commandery, made Qi grand palace grandee.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of Jianwu the emperor visited Wei commandery in person and named Qi grand palace grandee.

sentences.zh: 從還洛陽，又拜□尉。

translations.literal: Followed returned Luoyang, again appointed minister of guards commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On the return to Luoyang he was again appointed minister of the guards.

sentences.zh: 期重於信義，自為將，有所降下，未嘗虜掠。

translations.literal: Qi heavy in trust righteousness, self as general, those surrendered down, never plundered.
translations.idiomatic: Qi set great store by good faith: as a general he never looted those who yielded.

sentences.zh: 及在朝廷，憂國愛主，其有不得於心，必犯顏諫諍。

translations.literal: And at court, worried state loved ruler, those not obtaining in heart, must risk face remonstrate.
translations.idiomatic: At court he anguished for the state and loved his sovereign; whatever sat ill with him he would speak bluntly against the ruler's will.

sentences.zh: 帝嘗輕與期門近出，[一]期頓首車前曰：「臣聞古今之戒，變生不意，誠不願陛下微行數出。

translations.literal: Emperor once lightly with Qimen guard went out nearby, [one] Qi knocked head before carriage saying: "Your subject hears ancient modern's warning, change arises not from expectation, truly not wish Your Majesty slight travel several go.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor once slipped out incognito with only his Qimen guards [one]; Qi kowtowed before the chariot and said, "I have heard that sudden turns spare no man, ancient or modern; I beg Your Majesty not to make a habit of these secret excursions."

sentences.zh: 」帝為之回輿而還。

translations.literal: " Emperor for it turned carriage and returned.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor turned the carriage round and went back for his sake.

sentences.zh: 十年卒，[二]帝親臨襚斂，贈以□尉、安成侯印綬，謚曰忠侯。

translations.literal: Tenth year died, [two] emperor personally arrived bestow shroud, presented with minister of guards commandant, Ancheng marquis seals and ribbons, posthumous title said Loyal Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: He died in the tenth year [two]; the emperor came in person to lay the burial shroud and presented the seals of minister of the guards and of marquis of Ancheng, with the posthumous title Loyal Marquis.

sentences.zh: 注[一]前書，武帝將出，必與北地良家子期於殿門，故曰「期門」。

translations.literal: Note [one] Former Documents, Emperor Wu about exit, always with Beidi good family sons "appoint at palace gate", therefore called "Qimen".
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: The Former Han Documents say that Emperor Wu, before going out, always set a rendezvous with picked sons of good families from Beidi at the palace gate, hence the name "Qimen", gate of the rendezvous.

sentences.zh: 注[二]東觀記曰：「期疾病，使使者存問，加賜醫藥甚厚。

translations.literal: Note [two] Eastern View Records says: "Qi illness, sent envoy inquire, added bestowed medicine very thick."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [two]: The Eastern View Records says that when Qi fell ill the court sent an envoy to inquire and heaped medical gifts on him.

sentences.zh: 其母問期當封何子？

translations.literal: His mother asked Qi ought enfeoff which son?
translations.idiomatic: His mother asked which son should receive the enfeoffment?

sentences.zh: 期言『受國家恩深，常籩負，如死，不知當何以報國，何宜封子也』！

translations.literal: Qi said "Received state's favor deep, always fear unable repay, if die, not know ought how repay state, what suitable enfeoff son!"
translations.idiomatic: Qi replied, "I have received too much from the state and always fear I cannot repay it; if I die, I do not know how I could still repay it, how could I think of enfeoffing a son!"

sentences.zh: 上甚憐之。」

translations.literal: Emperor greatly pitied him."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was deeply moved."

sentences.zh: 子丹嗣。

translations.literal: Son Dan succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Tiao Dan inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 復封丹弟統為建平侯[13]。

translations.literal: Again enfeoffed Dan's younger brother Tong as Jianping marquis [13].
translations.idiomatic: Dan's younger brother Tiao Tong was also enfeoffed as marquis of Jianping [13].

sentences.zh: 後徙封丹葛陵侯[14]。

translations.literal: Afterward transferred enfeoffment Dan to Geleng marquis [14].
translations.idiomatic: Later Dan's fief was moved to Geleng [14].

sentences.zh: 丹卒，子舒嗣。

translations.literal: Dan died, son Shu succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Dan died, his son Shu succeeded.

sentences.zh: 舒卒，子羽嗣。

translations.literal: Shu died, son Yu succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Shu died, his son Yu succeeded.

sentences.zh: 羽卒，子蔡嗣。

translations.literal: Yu died, son Cai succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Yu died, his son Cai succeeded.

sentences.zh: 王霸字符伯，穎川穎陽人也。

translations.literal: Wang Ba styled Yuanbo, Yingchuan Yingyang man.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ba, courtesy name Yuanbo, was a man of Yingyang in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 世好文法，[一]父為郡決曹掾，[二]霸亦少為獄吏。

translations.literal: Generations loved documents law, [one] father was commandery sentencing bureau clerk, [two] Ba also in youth was prison clerk.
translations.idiomatic: For generations the family had loved statute and precedent [one]; his father had been sentencing clerk of the commandery [two], and Ba himself began as a prison clerk.

sentences.zh: 常慷慨不樂吏職，其父奇之。

translations.literal: Always stirred generous not happy clerk duty, his father marveled him.
translations.idiomatic: He was always restless and discontented with clerical work, and his father thought him unusual.

sentences.zh: 遣西學長安。

translations.literal: Sent west study Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: He was sent west to study in Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 漢兵起，光武過穎陽，霸率賓客上謁，曰：「將軍興義兵，竊不自知量，貪慕威德，願充行伍。

translations.literal: Han troops rose, Guangwu passed Yingyang, Ba led retainers ascended paid court, saying: "General raises righteous troops, I steal not self-know measure, covet admire awe virtue, wish fill ranks.
translations.idiomatic: When Han forces rose and Guangwu passed through Yingyang, Wang Ba led his clients to pay court, saying, "You raise the standard of righteousness; I know I overreach, yet I crave your majesty and virtue and beg a place in the ranks."

sentences.zh: 」光武曰：「夢想賢士，共成功業，豈有二哉！

translations.literal: " Guangwu said: "Dreamed sought worthy knights, together complete achievement enterprise, how can have two?
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu answered, "I have longed for worthy men to finish the great work with me; could I refuse you?"

sentences.zh: 」遂從擊破王尋、王邑於昆陽，還休鄉里。

translations.literal: " Thereupon followed struck Wang Xun, Wang Yi at Kunyang, returned rested village.
translations.idiomatic: He then followed the victory over Wang Xun and Wang Yi at Kunyang and went home to rest.

sentences.zh: 注[一]東觀記曰：「祖父為詔獄丞。」

translations.literal: Note [one] Eastern View Records says: "Grandfather was imperial prison assistant."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: The Eastern View Records says his grandfather had been assistant of the imperial prison.

sentences.zh: 注[二]漢舊儀：「決曹，主罪法事。」

translations.literal: Note [two] Han old regulations: "Sentencing bureau, in charge crime law affairs."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [two]: Han old regulations: the sentencing bureau handled penal law.

sentences.zh: 及光武為司隸校尉，道過穎陽，霸請其父，願從。

translations.literal: When Guangwu was metropolitan commandant, road passed Yingyang, Ba requested his father, wished follow.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu became the metropolitan commandant and passed Yingyang again, Wang Ba begged his father's leave to follow him.

sentences.zh: 父曰：「吾老矣，不任軍旅，汝往，勉之！

translations.literal: Father said: "I old, not bear army travel, you go, strive!
translations.idiomatic: His father said, "I am too old for campaigning; you go, and give it your all!"

sentences.zh: 」霸從至洛陽。

translations.literal: ' Ba followed reached Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ba followed him to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 及光武為大司馬，以霸為功曹令史，從度河北。

translations.literal: When Guangwu was grand marshal, made Ba merit bureau clerk-scribe, followed crossed Hebei.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu became grand marshal he made Wang Ba clerk-scribe of the merit bureau and took him north of the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 賓客從霸者數十人，稍稍引去。

translations.literal: Retainers who followed Ba several tens of men, gradually gradually drew away departed.
translations.idiomatic: Several dozen of Wang Ba's clients had followed him, but they drifted away one by one.

sentences.zh: 光武謂霸曰：「穎川從我者皆逝，而子獨留。

translations.literal: Guangwu told Ba saying: "Yingchuan those who followed me all gone, yet you alone remain.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu said to him, "The men of Yingchuan who followed me have all left; only you remain."

sentences.zh: 努力！

translations.literal: Strive hard!
translations.idiomatic: Strive hard!

sentences.zh: 疾風知勁草。」

translations.literal: Gale wind knows tough grass."
translations.idiomatic: In a high wind one sees which grass stands firm."

sentences.zh: 及王郎起，光武在薊，郎移檄購光武。

translations.literal: When Wang Lang rose, Guangwu at Ji, Lang moved proclamation purchase Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Lang rose, Guangwu was at Ji; Wang Lang put a price on his head.

sentences.zh: 光武令霸至市中募人，將以擊郎。

translations.literal: Guangwu ordered Ba go market within recruit men, will use strike Lang.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu ordered Wang Ba into the market to recruit men for the strike at Wang Lang.

sentences.zh: 市人皆大笑，舉手邪揄之，[一]霸慚懅而還。

translations.literal: Market people all great laughed, raised hands mock him, [one] Ba ashamed fearful returned.
translations.idiomatic: The townsfolk only laughed and mocked him with waves of the hand [one]; Wang Ba returned ashamed and shaken.

sentences.zh: [二]光武即南馳至下曲陽。

translations.literal: [two] Guangwu then south galloped reached Xiaquyang.
translations.idiomatic: [Two] Guangwu thereupon spurred south to Xiaquyang.

sentences.zh: 傳聞王郎兵在後，從者皆恐。

translations.literal: Transmitted rumor Wang Lang troops behind, followers all feared.
translations.idiomatic: Rumor said Wang Lang's army was on their heels, and the party was afraid.

sentences.zh: 及至虖沱河，候吏還白河水流澌，[三]無船，不可濟。

translations.literal: When reached Hutuo River, waiting clerk returned reported river water flowing ice-chips, [three] no boats, not fordable.
translations.idiomatic: At the Hutuo the scout came back reporting ice floes in the flood [three], no boats, no crossing.

sentences.zh: 官屬大懼。

translations.literal: Officials greatly feared.
translations.idiomatic: The staff were terrified.

sentences.zh: 光武令霸往視之。

translations.literal: Guangwu ordered Ba go inspect it.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu sent Wang Ba to look for himself.

sentences.zh: 霸恐驚觿，欲且前，阻水，還即詭曰：「冰堅可度。」

translations.literal: Ba feared frighten masses, wished for the nonce advance, block water, return then falsely said: "Ice firm can cross."
translations.idiomatic: Fearing panic, Wang Ba wished to press on; he blocked the bad news and lied on his return, "The ice is solid enough to cross."

sentences.zh: 官屬皆喜。

translations.literal: Officials all rejoiced.
translations.idiomatic: The staff rejoiced.

sentences.zh: 光武笑曰：「候吏果妄語也。

translations.literal: Guangwu laughed saying: "Waiting clerk indeed false words.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu laughed, "The scout was lying after all."

sentences.zh: 」遂前。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon advanced.
translations.idiomatic: They went forward.

sentences.zh: 比至河，河冰亦合，乃令霸護度，[四]未畢數騎而冰解。

translations.literal: When reached river, river ice also joined, then ordered Ba escort crossing, [four] not finished several riders then ice broke.
translations.idiomatic: When they reached the river the ice had bridged it; Guangwu had Wang Ba supervise the crossing [four], and before the last riders were over the ice broke up.

sentences.zh: 光武謂霸曰：「安吾觿得濟免者，卿之力也。

translations.literal: Guangwu told Ba saying: "Calmed my masses obtained ford escaped, your strength.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu said to him, "You steadied our men and got us across; the credit is yours."

sentences.zh: 」霸謝曰：「此明公至德，神靈之佑，雖武王白魚之應，無以加此。

translations.literal: " Ba declined saying: "This illustrious lord utmost virtue, spirits' blessing, although King Wu white fish omen, nothing surpass this.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ba demurred, "It is your supreme virtue and the blessing of the spirits; even King Wu's white-fish omen could not match this."

sentences.zh: 」[五]武光謂官屬曰：「王霸權以濟事，殆天瑞也。

translations.literal: " [five] Guangwu told officials saying: "Wang Ba expedient to complete affair, almost Heaven's auspicious sign.
translations.idiomatic: [Five] Guangwu told his staff, "Wang Ba bent the truth to save the day; surely that is an omen from Heaven."

sentences.zh: 」以為軍正，爵關內侯。

translations.literal: " Made army rectifier, noble rank marquis within the passes.
translations.idiomatic: He named him army rectifier with rank as marquis within the passes.

sentences.zh: 既至信都，發兵攻拔邯鄲。

translations.literal: Already reached Xindu, raised troops attacked took Handan.
translations.idiomatic: At Xindu he raised troops and took Handan.

sentences.zh: 霸追斬王郎，得其璽綬。

translations.literal: Ba pursued beheaded Wang Lang, obtained his seal ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ba ran Wang Lang down, beheaded him, and seized his seals and ribbons.

sentences.zh: 封王鄉侯。

translations.literal: Enfeoffed Wang township marquis.
translations.idiomatic: He was enfeoffed as marquis of Wang township.

sentences.zh: 注[一]說文曰：「歋□，手相笑也。

translations.literal: Note [one] Shuowen says: "Ye (manuscript lacuna), hands mutually laugh.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: The Shuowen jiezi glosses the expression as hands mocking one another in laughter.

sentences.zh: 」歋音弋支反。

translations.literal: " Ye sound yi-zhi fan.
translations.idiomatic: The graph ye is read with the yi-zhi fan spelling.

sentences.zh: □音踰，或音由。

translations.literal: (manuscript lacuna) sound yu, or sound you.
translations.idiomatic: The second graph is read like yu, or sometimes like you.

sentences.zh: 此云「邪揄」，語輕重不同。

translations.literal: This says "xieyu", language light-heavy not same.
translations.idiomatic: Here the text writes xieyu; the wording varies slightly in other sources.

sentences.zh: 注[二]懅亦籩也，音遽。

translations.literal: Note [two] Ju also means fear, sound ju.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [two]: Ju here means fear, read like ju.

sentences.zh: 注[三]澌音斯。

translations.literal: Note [three] Si sound si.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [three]: The graph si is read si.

sentences.zh: 注[四]監護度也。

translations.literal: Note [four] Supervise protect crossing.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [four]: Meaning he supervised the crossing.

sentences.zh: 注[五]今文尚書曰：「武王度盟津，白魚躍入王舟。」

translations.literal: Note [five] Modern-text Documents says: "King Wu crossed Meng Ford, white fish leaped into king's boat."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [five]: The modern-text Book of Documents records that when King Wu crossed the ford at Mengjin a white fish leapt into his boat.

sentences.zh: 從平河北，常與臧宮、傅俊共營，霸獨善撫士卒，死者脫衣以斂之，傷者躬親以養之。

translations.literal: Followed pacified Hebei, always with Zang Gong, Fu Jun shared camp, Ba alone good comforted soldiers, dead stripped clothes to bury them, wounded personally to nurture them.
translations.idiomatic: During the pacification of Hebei he often shared a camp with Zang Gong and Fu Jun, yet Wang Ba alone excelled at caring for the troops: he stripped his own clothes to shroud the dead and nursed the wounded with his own hands.

sentences.zh: 光武即位，以霸曉兵愛士，可獨任，拜為偏將軍，並將臧宮、傅俊兵，而以宮、俊為騎都尉。

translations.literal: Guangwu ascended throne, because Ba understood soldiers loved men, could alone entrust, appointed lieutenant general, together commanded Zang Gong, Fu Jun troops, while made Gong, Jun cavalry commandants.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu took the throne he judged Wang Ba fit to command alone because he knew war and cherished his men; he named him lieutenant general, gave him joint command of Zang Gong's and Fu Jun's troops, and made Gong and Jun cavalry commandants under him.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，更封富波侯。

translations.literal: Jianwu second year, again enfeoffed Fubo marquis.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Jianwu his fief was moved to Fubo.

translations.literal: [one]
translations.idiomatic: Endnote marker [one] in the received text.

sentences.zh: 注[一]富波，縣名，屬汝南郡，在今豫州。

translations.literal: Note [one] Fubo, county name, belonged Runan commandery, at today Yuzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: Fubo was a county in Runan commandery, in the area of present-day Yuzhou.

sentences.zh: 四年秋，帝幸譙，使霸與捕虜將軍馬武東討周建於垂惠。

translations.literal: Fourth year autumn, emperor visited Qiao, ordered Ba with campaign Caitiffs general Ma Wu east punish Zhou Jian at Chuihui.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the fourth year the emperor went to Qiao and sent Wang Ba with Ma Wu, general who campaigns against the Caitiffs, east against Zhou Jian at Chuihui.

sentences.zh: 蘇茂將五校兵四千餘人救建，而先遣精騎遮擊馬武軍糧，武往救之。

translations.literal: Su Mao led five camps troops more than four thousand save Jian, while first dispatched crack cavalry intercept strike Ma Wu army grain, Wu went save it.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao brought more than four thousand men of the five camps to relieve Zhou Jian and first sent picked cavalry to cut Ma Wu's supply line; Ma Wu went to the rescue.

sentences.zh: 建從城中出兵夾擊武，武恃霸之援，戰不甚力，為茂、建所敗。

translations.literal: Jian from city within dispatched troops pincer strike Wu, Wu relied on Ba's aid, battle not very forceful, by Mao, Jian defeated.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Jian sallied from the city to catch Ma Wu in a pincer; Ma Wu, counting on Wang Ba's support, did not fight at full stretch and was beaten by Su Mao and Zhou Jian.

sentences.zh: 武軍奔過霸營，大呼求救。

translations.literal: Wu army ran past Ba camp, great shouted beg save.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Wu's men raced past Wang Ba's camp shouting for help.

sentences.zh: 霸曰：「賊兵盛，出必兩敗，努力而已。

translations.literal: Ba said: "Bandit troops flourishing, exit must two defeat, strive only.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ba said, 'The enemy is strong; if we go out both of us will lose—hold fast.'

sentences.zh: 」乃閉營堅壁。

translations.literal: " Then closed camp strengthened walls.
translations.idiomatic: He shut the camp and strengthened the walls.

sentences.zh: 軍吏皆爭之。

translations.literal: Army clerks all disputed him.
translations.idiomatic: His officers all remonstrated with him.

sentences.zh: 霸曰：「茂兵精銳，其觿又多，吾吏士心恐，而捕虜與吾相恃，兩軍不一，此敗道也。

translations.literal: Ba said: "Mao's troops crack sharp, his masses also many, our clerks soldiers heart afraid, while Caitiff-catcher and me mutually rely, two armies not one, this defeat's way.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ba said, 'Su Mao's men are picked and numerous; our troops are afraid. The Caitiff-catcher and I depend on each other—if the two hosts do not act as one, that is the road to defeat.'

sentences.zh: 今閉營固守，示不相援，賊必乘勝輕進；

translations.literal: Now close camp firm guard, show not mutually aid, bandits must ride victory light advance;
translations.idiomatic: Shut the camp and stand firm, show that we will not lend aid, and the enemy will press their advantage and advance rashly;

sentences.zh: 捕虜無救，其戰自倍。

translations.literal: Caitiff-catcher no rescue, his battle self double.
translations.idiomatic: with no rescue for Ma Wu, his men will fight twice as hard.

sentences.zh: 如此，茂觿疲勞，吾承其弊，乃可克也。

translations.literal: Like this, Mao masses weary tired, I receive their exhaustion, then can overcome.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao's host will tire; I will strike their exhaustion and can win.'

sentences.zh: 」茂、建果悉出攻武。

translations.literal: " Mao, Jian indeed all out attacked Wu.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao and Zhou Jian threw their whole strength against Ma Wu.

sentences.zh: 合戰良久，霸軍中壯士路潤等數十人斷髮請戰。

translations.literal: Joined battle long time, Ba army within stalwart Lu Run and others several tens men cut hair beg battle.
translations.idiomatic: The fight wore on until several dozen stalwarts in Wang Ba's ranks, Lu Run among them, cut their hair and begged to be sent in.

sentences.zh: 霸知士心銳，乃開營後，出精騎襲其背。

translations.literal: Ba knew soldiers' hearts sharp, then opened camp rear, sent crack cavalry raid their back.
translations.idiomatic: Reading the men's fighting spirit, Wang Ba opened the rear of his camp and sent picked cavalry against the enemy's rear.

sentences.zh: 茂、建前後受敵，驚亂敗走，霸、武各歸營。

translations.literal: Mao, Jian front rear received enemy, alarm chaos defeated fled, Ba, Wu each returned camp.
translations.idiomatic: Caught front and rear, Su Mao and Zhou Jian broke in panic; Wang Ba and Ma Wu each drew back to camp.

sentences.zh: 賊復聚觿挑戰，霸堅臥不出，方饗士作倡樂。

translations.literal: Bandits again gathered masses challenge battle, Ba firm lay not out, just feasted soldiers made music.
translations.idiomatic: The rebels massed again and offered battle; Wang Ba stayed shut in his camp and feasted his men to music.

sentences.zh: 茂雨射營中，中霸前酒樽，霸安坐不動。

translations.literal: Mao rained shoot into camp, hit Ba front wine goblet, Ba sat calm not moved.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao showered the camp with arrows and struck the wine cup before Wang Ba; Wang Ba sat unmoved.

sentences.zh: 軍吏皆曰：「茂前日已破；

translations.literal: Army clerks all said: "Mao other day already broken;
translations.idiomatic: His officers said, 'Su Mao was beaten the other day—'

sentences.zh: 今易擊也。

translations.literal: now easy strike.
translations.idiomatic: he should be easy meat now.'

sentences.zh: 」霸曰：「不然。

translations.literal: " Ba said: "Not so.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ba said, 'No.'

sentences.zh: 蘇茂客兵遠來，糧食不足，故數挑戰，以儌一切之勝。

translations.literal: Su Mao guest troops far come, grain food not enough, therefore several times challenge battle, to seek one-moment's victory.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao's auxiliaries have come a long way and are short of food; that is why he keeps challenging us, angling for a lucky stroke.'

sentences.zh: [一]今閉營休士，所謂不戰而屈人之兵，善之善者也。

translations.literal: [one] Now close camp rest soldiers, so-called not battle yet bend others' troops, good within good.
translations.idiomatic: [One] To rest the army behind closed gates is the best of the best—to win without fighting.'

sentences.zh: 」茂、建既不得戰，乃引還營。

translations.literal: " Mao, Jian already not obtain battle, then led return camp.
translations.idiomatic: Unable to bring him to battle, Su Mao and Zhou Jian withdrew to camp.

sentences.zh: 其夜，建兄子誦反，閉城拒之，茂、建遁去，誦以城降。

translations.literal: That night, Jian's elder brother's son Song rebelled, closed city refused them, Mao, Jian fled departed, Song with city surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: That night Zhou Jian's nephew Zhou Song mutinied, barred the gates against them, and Su Mao and Zhou Jian fled; Zhou Song surrendered the city.

sentences.zh: 注[一]儌，要也。

translations.literal: Note [one] Jiao means seek.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: Jiao here means to seek or angle for.

sentences.zh: 一切猶權時也。

translations.literal: One-moment still means expedient time.
translations.idiomatic: One-moment means a temporary or opportune stroke.

sentences.zh: 五年春，帝使太中大夫持節拜霸為討虜將軍。

translations.literal: Fifth year spring, emperor ordered grand palace grandee hold tally appoint Ba campaign Caitiffs general.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the fifth year the emperor sent a grand palace grandee with credentials to name Wang Ba general who campaigns against the Caitiffs.

sentences.zh: 六年，屯田新安。

translations.literal: Sixth year, garrison-field farmed Xin'an.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year he opened tuntian fields at Xin'an.

sentences.zh: 八年，屯*[田]*函谷關。

translations.literal: Eighth year, garrisoned *field* Hangu Pass.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year he garrisoned the Hangu Pass.

sentences.zh: 擊滎陽、中牟盜賊，皆平之。

translations.literal: Struck Xingyang, Zhongmou bandits, all pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: He reduced the bandits of Xingyang and Zhongmou and pacified them.

sentences.zh: 九年，霸與吳漢及橫野大將軍王常、建義大將軍朱佑、破奸將軍侯進等五萬餘人，擊盧芳將賈覽、閔堪於高柳。

translations.literal: Ninth year, Ba with Wu Han and Field general Wang Chang, Establish Righteousness Zhu You, Break Treachery Hou Jin and others more than fifty thousand, struck Lu Fang's generals Jia Lan, Min Kan at Gaoliu.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year Wang Ba joined Wu Han, Wang Chang the field general, Zhu You who establishes righteousness, Hou Jin who breaks treachery, and more than fifty thousand others against Lu Fang's generals Jia Lan and Min Kan at Gaoliu.

sentences.zh: 匈奴遣騎助芳，漢車遇雨，戰不利。

translations.literal: Xiongnu dispatched horse aid Fang, Han army met rain, battle not favorable.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu sent cavalry to help Lu Fang; the Han army was caught in the rain and the battle went badly.

sentences.zh: 吳漢還洛陽，令朱佑屯常山，王常屯涿郡，侯進屯漁陽。

translations.literal: Wu Han returned Luoyang, ordered Zhu You garrison Changshan, Wang Chang garrison Zhuo commandery, Hou Jin garrison Yuyang.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han went back to Luoyang while Zhu You was ordered to hold Changshan, Wang Chang Zhuo commandery, and Hou Jin Yuyang.

sentences.zh: 璽書拜霸上谷太守，領屯兵如故，捕擊胡虜，無拘郡界。

translations.literal: Imperial edict appointed Ba Shanggu governor, lead garrison troops as before, capture strike Hu caitiffs, not limited commandery border.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial rescript named him governor of Shanggu, keeping his garrison command as before, with authority to pursue Hu raiders without respect to commandery boundaries.

sentences.zh: [一]明年，霸復與吳漢等四將軍六萬人出高柳擊賈覽，詔霸與漁陽太守陳欣將兵為諸軍鋒。

translations.literal: [one] Next year, Ba again with Wu Han and four other generals sixty thousand men exited Gaoliu strike Jia Lan, edict Ba with Yuyang governor Chen Xin lead troops as various armies' point.
translations.idiomatic: [One] The next year Wang Ba again joined Wu Han and four other generals with sixty thousand men out of Gaoliu against Jia Lan; the edict told Wang Ba and Yuyang governor Chen Xin to lead the van of the combined armies.

sentences.zh: 匈奴左南將軍將數千騎救覽，霸等連戰於平城下，破之，追出塞，斬首數百級。

translations.literal: Xiongnu left south general led several thousand horse save Lan, Ba and others continuous battles at Pingcheng below, defeated them, pursued out barrier, beheaded heads several hundred grades.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu left south general came with several thousand horsemen to relieve Jia Lan; Wang Ba and his colleagues fought them repeatedly below Pingcheng, broke them, and pursued beyond the passes for several hundred heads.

sentences.zh: 霸及諸將還入鴈門，與驃騎大將軍杜茂會攻盧芳將尹由於崞、繁畤，不克。

translations.literal: Ba and various generals returned entered Yanmen, with valiant cavalry grand general Du Mao joined attack Lu Fang's general Yin You at Guo, Fanzhi, not took.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ba and the generals re-entered Yanmen and with Du Mao, grand general of valiant cavalry, attacked Lu Fang's officer Yin You at Guo and Fanzhi but could not carry the places.

translations.literal: [two]
translations.idiomatic: Endnote marker [two] in the received text.

sentences.zh: 注[一]拘猶限也。

translations.literal: Note [one] Ju still means limit.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: Ju here means to confine or limit.

sentences.zh: 注[二]崞及繁畤皆縣名，屬鴈門郡，並今代州縣也，有崞山焉。

translations.literal: Note [two] Guo and Fanzhi both county names, belonged Yanmen commandery, both today Daizhou counties, there is Guo mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [two]: Guo and Fanzhi were counties in Yanmen commandery, both in the present Daizhou region, near Mount Guo.

sentences.zh: 崞音郭。

translations.literal: Guo sound guo.
translations.idiomatic: Guo is read like guo.

sentences.zh: 十三年，增邑戶，更封向侯。

translations.literal: Thirteenth year, increased fief households, again enfeoffed Xiang marquis.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirteenth year his fief was enlarged and he was transferred to the marquisate of Xiang.

sentences.zh: [一]是時，盧芳與匈奴、烏桓連兵，寇盜尤數，緣邊愁苦。

translations.literal: [one] At that time, Lu Fang with Xiongnu, Wuhuan linked troops, raided stole especially many, along border sorrow bitter.
translations.idiomatic: [One] Lu Fang was then in league with the Xiongnu and Wuhuan; raids were constant and the frontier suffered.

sentences.zh: 詔霸將□刑徒六千餘人，與杜茂治飛狐道，[二]堆石布土，築起亭障，自代至平城三百餘里。

translations.literal: Edict Ba lead remitted convict laborers more than six thousand men, with Du Mao repair Flying Fox road, [two] pile stone spread earth, build rise watchtowers barriers, from Dai to Pingcheng more than three hundred li.
translations.idiomatic: By edict Wang Ba led more than six thousand remitted convict laborers with Du Mao to repair the Flying Fox road, [two] piling stone and earth to raise watchtowers and walls for more than three hundred li from Dai to Pingcheng.

sentences.zh: 凡與匈奴、烏桓大小數十百戰，頗識邊事，數上書言宜與匈奴結和親，又陳委輸可從溫水漕，[三]以省陸轉輸之勞，事皆施行。

translations.literal: In all with Xiongnu, Wuhuan large small several tens hundred battles, quite understood border affairs, several times submitted memorial spoke ought with Xiongnu tie peace kinship, again presented transport could follow Warm Water canal, [three] to save land transport relay's toil, affairs all implemented carried out.
translations.idiomatic: He fought the Xiongnu and Wuhuan dozens or hundreds of times, large and small, and knew the frontier well; he repeatedly memorialized for peace and marriage with the Xiongnu and proposed shipping supplies up the Warm Water canal [three] to spare the labor of overland haulage—measures that were adopted.

sentences.zh: 後南單于、烏桓降服，北邊無事。

translations.literal: After south Chanyu, Wuhuan submitted submit, north border without affair.
translations.idiomatic: After the southern chanyu and the Wuhuan submitted, the north was quiet.

sentences.zh: 霸在上谷二十餘歲。

translations.literal: Ba at Shanggu more than twenty years.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ba spent more than twenty years at Shanggu.

sentences.zh: 三十年，定封淮陵侯。

translations.literal: Thirtieth year, fixed enfeoffment Huailing marquis.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirtieth year his title was fixed as marquis of Huailing.

sentences.zh: [四] 永平二年，以病免，後數月卒。

translations.literal: [four] Yongping second year, by illness dismissed, after several months died.
translations.idiomatic: [Four] In the second year of Yongping he stepped down for illness and died a few months later.

sentences.zh: 注[一]向，縣名，屬沛郡。

translations.literal: Note [one] Xiang, county name, belonged Pei commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: Xiang was a county in Pei commandery.

sentences.zh: 左傳曰：「莒人入向。

translations.literal: Zuo Commentary says: "Ju people entered Xiang.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Commentary says, 'The people of Ju entered Xiang.'

sentences.zh: 」案：今密州莒縣南又有向城。

translations.literal: " Case: today Mizhou Ju county south again has Xiang city.
translations.idiomatic: Note: south of present-day Ju county in Mizhou there is another town called Xiang.

sentences.zh: 注[二]飛狐道在今蔚州飛狐縣，北通媯州懷戎縣，即古之飛狐口也。

translations.literal: Note [two] Flying Fox road at today Weizhou Flying Fox county, north connects Guizhou Huairong county, namely ancient Flying Fox mouth.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [two]: The Flying Fox road ran through present-day Flying Fox county in Weizhou, northward to Huairong in Guizhou—the ancient Flying Fox defile.

sentences.zh: 注[三]水經注曰，溫余水出上谷居庸關東，又東過軍都縣南，又東過薊縣北。

translations.literal: Note [three] Shuijing zhu says, Warm remainder water exits Shanggu Juyong pass east, again east passed Junjun county south, again east passed Ji county north.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [three]: The Commentary on the Water Classic traces the Warm Remainder River from east of Juyong pass in Shanggu east past Jun county south and Ji county north.

sentences.zh: 益通以運漕也。

translations.literal: Increase open to transport canal.
translations.idiomatic: Widening it would ease canal transport.

sentences.zh: 注[四]淮陵，縣，屬臨淮郡。

translations.literal: Note [four] Huailing, county, belonged Linhuai commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [four]: Huailing was a county in Linhuai commandery.

sentences.zh: 子符嗣，徙封軑侯。

translations.literal: Son Fu succeeded, transferred enfeoffment Zhi marquis.
translations.idiomatic: His son Wang Fu inherited and was later moved to the marquisate of Zhi.

sentences.zh: [一]符卒，子度嗣。

translations.literal: [one] Fu died, son Du succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: [One] When Fu died, his son Du succeeded.

sentences.zh: 度尚顯宗女浚儀長公主，為黃門郎。

translations.literal: Du married Emperor Ming's daughter senior princess of Junyi, was Yellow Gate gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: Du married Emperor Ming's daughter, the senior princess of Junyi, and served as a gentleman of the Yellow Gates.

sentences.zh: 度卒，子歆嗣。

translations.literal: Du died, son Xin succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Du died, his son Xin inherited.

sentences.zh: 注[一]軑，縣，屬江夏郡。

translations.literal: Note [one] Zhi, county, belonged Jiangxia commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: Zhi was a county in Jiangxia commandery.

sentences.zh: 軑音大。

translations.literal: Zhi sound dai.
translations.idiomatic: Zhi is read like dai.

sentences.zh: 祭遵及祭肜

translations.literal: Ji Zun and Ji Rong
translations.idiomatic: The text turns to Zhai Zun and Zhai Rong.

sentences.zh: 祭遵字弟孫，[一]穎川穎陽人也。

translations.literal: Ji Zun styled Disun, [one] Yingchuan Yingyang man.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zun, courtesy name Disun, [one] was a man of Yingyang in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 少好經書。

translations.literal: In youth loved classics books.
translations.idiomatic: From boyhood he loved the classical texts.

sentences.zh: 家富給，而遵恭儉，惡衣服。

translations.literal: Family rich ample, yet Zun respectful frugal, hated fine clothes.
translations.idiomatic: His household was wealthy, yet Zhai Zun was modest and plain and disdained fine dress.

sentences.zh: 喪母，負土起墳。

translations.literal: Mother died, carried earth raised tomb.
translations.idiomatic: When his mother died he carried earth to build her tomb.

sentences.zh: 嘗為部吏所侵，結客殺之。

translations.literal: Once was department clerk bullied, gathered retainers killed him.
translations.idiomatic: A department clerk had once wronged him; he gathered clients and killed the man.

sentences.zh: 初，縣中以其柔也，既而皆憚焉。

translations.literal: At first, county within because him soft, afterward all feared him.
translations.idiomatic: At first the county thought him mild; afterward everyone feared him.

sentences.zh: 注[一]祭音側界反。

translations.literal: Note [one] Ji sound ce-jie fan.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: The surname Ji is read with the ce-jie fan spelling.

sentences.zh: 及光武破王尋等，還過穎陽，遵以縣吏數進見，光武愛其容儀，署為門下史。

translations.literal: When Guangwu defeated Wang Xun and others, returned passed Yingyang, Zun as county clerk several times advanced audience, Guangwu loved his appearance bearing, appointed gate clerk-scribe.
translations.idiomatic: After Guangwu broke Wang Xun and his host and passed Yingyang again, Zhai Zun, as a county clerk, was admitted several times; Guangwu admired his bearing and made him a gate clerk-scribe.

sentences.zh: 從征河北，為軍市令。

translations.literal: Followed campaigned Hebei, was army market commandant.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned north of the Yellow River as army market commandant.

sentences.zh: 捨中兒犯法，遵格殺之。

translations.literal: Barracks within lad violated law, Zun rule killed him.
translations.idiomatic: A boy from the headquarters broke the law and Zhai Zun had him executed on the spot.

sentences.zh: 光武怒，命收遵。

translations.literal: Guangwu angry, ordered arrest Zun.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu was furious and ordered Zhai Zun arrested.

sentences.zh: 時主簿陳副諫曰：「明公常欲觿軍整齊，今遵奉法不避，是教令所行也。

translations.literal: At that time chief clerk Chen Fu remonstrated saying: "Illustrious lord always desires masses army neat, now Zun upholds law not avoiding, is teaching order what is carried.
translations.idiomatic: Chief clerk Chen Fu remonstrated, 'You want the army kept in good order; Zhai Zun enforces the law without favor—that is exactly how orders are carried out.'

sentences.zh: 」光武乃貰之，[一] 以為刺奸將軍。

translations.literal: " Guangwu then pardoned him, [one] made thorn-traitor general.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu pardoned him [one] and named him general who probes treachery.

sentences.zh: 謂諸將曰：「當備祭遵！

translations.literal: Told various generals saying: "Must guard against Ji Zun!
translations.idiomatic: He told his generals, 'Watch yourselves around Zhai Zun—'

sentences.zh: 吾捨中兒犯法尚殺之，必不私諸卿也。

translations.literal: My barracks lad violated law still killed him, certainly not privately favor you gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: he killed even a boy from my own quarters for breaking the law; he will show you no private favor.'

sentences.zh: 」尋拜為偏將軍，從平河北，以功封列侯。

translations.literal: " Soon appointed lieutenant general, followed pacified Hebei, by merit enfeoffed full marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Soon he was made lieutenant general, followed the pacification of Hebei, and was enfeoffed a full marquis for his merit.

sentences.zh: 注[一]貰猶赦也。

translations.literal: Note [one] Shi still means pardon.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: Shi means to pardon.

sentences.zh: 建武二年春，拜征虜將軍，定封穎陽侯。

translations.literal: Jianwu second year spring, appointed campaign Caitiffs general, fixed enfeoffment Yingyang marquis.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the second year of Jianwu he was named general who campaigns against the Caitiffs with a fixed fief as marquis of Yingyang.

sentences.zh: 與驃騎大將軍景丹、建義大將軍朱佑、漢忠將軍王常、騎都尉王梁、臧宮等入箕關，[一]南擊弘農、厭新、柏華蠻中賊。

translations.literal: With valiant cavalry grand general Jing Dan, Establish Righteousness Zhu You, Han Loyalty Wang Chang, cavalry commandant Wang Liang, Zang Gong and others entered Ji Pass, [one] south struck Hongnong, Yanxin, Baihua Man within bandits.
translations.idiomatic: With Jing Dan, Zhu You, Wang Chang, Wang Liang, Zang Gong, and others he entered Ji Pass [one] and struck south against the Man bandits of Hongnong, Yanxin, and Baihua.

sentences.zh: [二]弩中遵口，洞出流血，觿見遵傷，稍引退，遵呼叱止之，士卒戰皆自倍，遂大破之。

translations.literal: [two] Crossbow hit Zun mouth, pierced through flowed blood, masses saw Zun wounded, gradually drew back, Zun shouted scolded stopped them, soldiers battle all self doubled, thereupon greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: [Two] A crossbow bolt pierced Zhai Zun's mouth and came out bleeding; the men, seeing him hurt, began to fall back until he roared them to a halt, and they fought twice as hard and routed the enemy.

sentences.zh: 時新城蠻中山賊張滿，[三]屯結險隘為人害，詔遵攻之。

translations.literal: At that time Xincheng Man Zhongshan bandit Zhang Man, [three] garrisoned joined peril defiles as men harm, edict Zun attack him.
translations.idiomatic: [Three] Zhang Man of the Zhongshan bandits in Xincheng Man was holding the defiles to plague the region; Zhai Zun was ordered against him.

sentences.zh: 遵絕其糧道，滿數挑戰，遵堅壁不出。

translations.literal: Zun cut his grain road, Man several times challenge battle, Zun firm walls not out.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zun severed his supply line; Zhang Man challenged again and again, but Zhai Zun stayed behind his walls.

sentences.zh: 而厭新、柏華餘賊復與滿合，遂攻得霍陽聚，[四]遵乃分兵擊破降之。

translations.literal: While Yanxin, Baihua remainder bandits again with Man joined, thereupon attacked took Huoyang gathering, [four] Zun then divided troops struck defeated surrendered them.
translations.idiomatic: The remnants of Yanxin and Baihua joined Zhang Man, took the Huoyang stockade, [four] until Zhai Zun divided his force, broke them, and received their surrender.

sentences.zh: 明年春，張滿饑困，城拔，生獲之。

translations.literal: Next year spring, Zhang Man hungry trapped, city pulled, alive captured him.
translations.idiomatic: The next spring, starving and trapped, Zhang Man lost his fort and was taken alive.

sentences.zh: 初，滿祭祀天地，自雲當王，既執，歎曰：「讖文誤我！

translations.literal: At first, Man sacrificed prayed Heaven Earth, self said ought king, already seized, sighed saying: "Prophetic text misled me!
translations.idiomatic: He had sacrificed to Heaven and Earth, claiming the omens made him king; when captured he sighed, "The prophecy texts misled me!"

sentences.zh: 」乃斬之，夷其妻子。

translations.literal: " Then beheaded him, exterminated his wife children.
translations.idiomatic: He was beheaded and his wife and children extirpated.

sentences.zh: 遵引兵南擊鄧奉弟終於杜衍，破之。

translations.literal: Zun led troops south struck Deng Feng younger brother Zhong at Duyan, defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zun marched south and defeated Deng Feng's younger brother Deng Zhong at Duyan.

translations.literal: [five]
translations.idiomatic: The chapter carries the marginal note [five] at this break in the narrative.

sentences.zh: 注[一]箕關，解在鄧禹傳。

translations.literal: Note [one] Ji Pass, explanation in Deng Yu biography.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [one]: Ji Pass is explained in the biography of Deng Yu.

sentences.zh: 注[二]東觀記曰柏華聚也。

translations.literal: Note [two] Eastern View Records says Baihua stockade.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [two]: The Eastern View Records identifies the place as the Baihua stockade.

sentences.zh: 注[三]新城，縣名，屬河南郡，今伊闕縣也。

translations.literal: Note [three] Xincheng, county name, belonged Henan commandery, today Yique county.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [three]: Xincheng was a county in Henan commandery, in the area of present-day Yique.

sentences.zh: 注[四]有霍陽山，故名焉，俗謂之張侯城，在今汝州西南。

translations.literal: Note [four] there is Huoyang mountain, therefore named, vulgarly called Marquis Zhang's fort, at today Ruzhou southwest.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [four]: It is named for Mount Huoyang; popular usage calls it Marquis Zhang's fort, southwest of present-day Nanyang in Dengzhou.

sentences.zh: 注[五]杜衍，縣名，屬南陽郡，故城在今鄧州南陽縣西南。

translations.literal: Note [five] Duyan, county name, belonged Nanyang commandery, old city at today Dengzhou Nanyang county southwest.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [five]: Duyan was a county in Nanyang commandery, southwest of present-day Nanyang in Dengzhou.

sentences.zh: 時涿郡太守張豐執使者舉兵反，自稱無上大將軍，與彭寵連兵。

translations.literal: At that time Zhuo commandery governor Zhang Feng seized envoy raised troops rebel, self-called unsurpassed great general, with Peng Chong linked troops.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Feng, governor of Zhuo, seized an imperial envoy, rose in arms, styled himself unsurpassed grand general, and joined forces with Peng Chong.

sentences.zh: 四年，遵與朱佑及建威大將軍耿弇﹑驍騎將軍劉喜俱擊之。

translations.literal: Fourth year, Zun with Zhu You and Establish Might grand general Geng Yan, Valiant Cavalry Liu Xi together struck them.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year Zhai Zun joined Zhu You, Geng Yan the grand general who establishes might, and Liu Xi the valiant cavalry general in the attack.

sentences.zh: 遵兵先至，急攻豐，豐功曹孟□執豐降。

translations.literal: Zun troops first arrived, urgent attacked Feng, Feng merit clerk Meng (manuscript lacuna) seized Feng surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zun's troops arrived first and stormed Zhang Feng; Feng's merit clerk Meng (name lacuna) seized Feng and surrendered him.

sentences.zh: [15]初，豐好方術，有道士言豐當為天子，以五彩囊裹石系豐肘，雲石中有玉璽。

translations.literal: [15] At first, Feng loved formulas techniques, there was Daoist who said Feng ought be Son of Heaven, with five-color pouch wrapped stone tied Feng's elbow, said stone within had jade seal.
translations.idiomatic: [15] Zhang Feng had dabbled in occult arts; a Daoist told him he was destined to be Son of Heaven, tied a five-colored bag holding a stone to his elbow, and claimed a jade seal lay inside the stone.

sentences.zh: 豐信之，遂反。

translations.literal: Feng believed it, thereupon rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Feng believed him and rebelled.

sentences.zh: 既執當斬，猶曰：「肘石有玉璽。

translations.literal: Already seized about behead, still said: "Elbow stone has jade seal.
translations.idiomatic: Even on the execution ground he insisted, "The stone on my elbow holds the jade seal."

sentences.zh: 」遵為椎破之，豐乃知被詐，仰天歎曰：「當死無所恨！

translations.literal: " Zun for him mallet broke it, Feng then knew deceived, looked up heaven sighed saying: "About die nothing to regret!
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zun smashed it with a mallet; Zhang Feng saw the fraud, looked to heaven, and said, "I deserve death and bear no grudge!"

sentences.zh: 」諸將皆引還，遵受詔留屯良鄉拒彭寵。

translations.literal: " Various generals all led returned, Zun received edict remain garrison Liangxiang resist Peng Chong.
translations.idiomatic: The other generals withdrew while Zhai Zun, by edict, stayed to hold Liangxiang against Peng Chong.

sentences.zh: 因遣護軍傅玄襲擊寵將李豪於潞，大破之，斬首千餘級。

translations.literal: Thereupon sent protector-of-the-army Fu Xuan surprise attack Chong's general Li Hao at Lu, greatly defeated them, beheaded heads more than thousand grades.
translations.idiomatic: He sent the protector of the army, Fu Xuan, to fall on Peng Chong's general Li Hao at Lu, routed him, and took more than a thousand heads.

sentences.zh: 相拒歲餘，數挫其鋒，黨與多降者。

translations.literal: Mutually opposed more than year, several times blunted their point, faction followers many surrendered ones.
translations.idiomatic: They faced each other for over a year; Zhai Zun repeatedly blunted the enemy's edge and many of their followers came over.

sentences.zh: 及寵死，遵進定其地。

translations.literal: When Chong died, Zun advanced settled that land.
translations.idiomatic: When Peng Chong died, Zhai Zun advanced and pacified the territory.

sentences.zh: 六年春，詔遵與建威大將軍耿弇﹑虎牙大將軍蓋延﹑漢忠將軍王常﹑捕虜將軍馬武﹑驍騎將軍劉歆﹑武威將軍劉尚等從天水伐公孫述。

translations.literal: Sixth year spring, edict ordered Zun with Establish Might grand general Geng Yan, Tiger-fang grand general Gai Yan, Han Loyalty Wang Chang, Caitiff-catcher Ma Wu, Valiant Cavalry Liu Xin, Martial Weiliance Liu Shang and others from Tianshui campaign Gongsun Shu.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the sixth year an edict ordered Zhai Zun to join Geng Yan, Gai Yan, Wang Chang, Ma Wu, Liu Xin, Liu Shang, and others from Tianshui in the campaign against Gongsun Shu.

sentences.zh: [16]師次長安，時車駕亦至，而隗囂不欲漢兵上隴，辭說解故。

translations.literal: [16] Army halted Chang'an, at that time imperial carriage also arrived, yet Wei Ao did not desire Han troops ascend Long, pleaded explained excuses.
translations.idiomatic: [16] The army halted at Chang'an as the emperor arrived, but Wei Ao refused to let Han forces up onto Long and pleaded excuses.

sentences.zh: [17]帝召諸將議。

translations.literal: [17] Emperor summoned various generals deliberated.
translations.idiomatic: [17] The emperor called the generals into council.

sentences.zh: 皆曰：「可且延囂日月之期，益封其將帥，以消散之。

translations.literal: All said: "Can for the nonce extend Ao sun-moon's period, increase enfeoff his generals commanders, by dissolve scatter them.
translations.idiomatic: They said, "We might stall Wei Ao for a time, enlarge the fiefs of his generals, and so break him up.'

sentences.zh: 」遵曰：「囂挾奸久矣。

translations.literal: " Zun said: "Ao harbors treachery long already.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zun said, 'Wei Ao has long nursed treachery.'

sentences.zh: 今若按甲引時，則使其詐謀益深，而蜀警增備，固不如遂進。

translations.literal: Now if press armor draw out time, then causes his deceitful plots yet deeper, while Shu alert increases preparation, firmly not as good as thereupon advance.
translations.idiomatic: To halt the army now would only deepen his plots and let Shu tighten its defenses—we had better strike at once.'

sentences.zh: 」帝從之，乃遣遵為前行。

translations.literal: " Emperor followed it, then dispatched Zun as forward march.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed and sent Zhai Zun ahead as vanguard.

sentences.zh: 隗囂使其將王元拒隴坻，遵進擊，破之，追至新關。

translations.literal: Wei Ao made his general Wang Yuan resist Long mound, Zun advanced attacked, defeated them, pursued to Xin Pass.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Ao posted Wang Yuan to hold the Long mound; Zhai Zun attacked, broke him, and chased him to Xin Pass.

sentences.zh: 及諸將到，與囂戰，並敗，引退下隴。

translations.literal: When various generals arrived, with Ao battled, together defeated, led withdraw down Long.
translations.idiomatic: When the full host came up they fought Wei Ao, were defeated together, and fell back off Long.

sentences.zh: 乃詔遵軍汧，耿弇軍漆，征西大將軍馮異軍栒邑，大司馬吳漢等還屯長安。

translations.literal: Thereupon edict ordered Zun army Qian, Geng Yan army Qi, Campaign West grand general Feng Yi army Xunyi, grand marshal Wu Han etc return garrison Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: An edict then stationed Zhai Zun at Qian, Geng Yan at Qi, Feng Yi the grand general who campaigns west at Xunyi, and Wu Han the grand marshal and others back to garrison Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 自是後遵數挫隗囂。

translations.literal: From this after Zun several times thwarted Wei Ao.
translations.idiomatic: Thereafter Zhai Zun repeatedly worsted Wei Ao.

sentences.zh: 事已見馮異傳。

translations.literal: Affair already seen Feng Yi biography.
translations.idiomatic: The details are given in the biography of Feng Yi.

sentences.zh: 八年秋，復從車駕上隴。

translations.literal: Eighth year autumn, again followed imperial carriage ascended Long.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the eighth year he again accompanied the emperor up onto Long.

sentences.zh: 及囂破，帝東歸過汧，幸遵營，勞饗士卒，作黃門武樂，良夜乃罷[18]。

translations.literal: When Ao broken, emperor east returned passed Qian, visited Zun camp, feasted comforted soldiers, made Yellow Gate martial music, fine night then ended [18].
translations.idiomatic: When Wei Ao fell the emperor returned east by way of Qian, visited Zhai Zun's camp, feasted the troops to Yellow Gate martial music, and kept the revel far into the night [18].

sentences.zh: 時遵有疾，詔賜重茵，覆以御蓋。

translations.literal: At that time Zun had illness, edict bestowed heavy mat, covered with imperial umbrella.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zun was ill; the court sent him a thick mat and covered him with the imperial umbrella.

sentences.zh: 復令進屯隴下。

translations.literal: Again ordered advance garrison below Long.
translations.idiomatic: He was ordered forward to garrison below Long.

sentences.zh: 及公孫述遣兵救囂，吳漢﹑耿弇等悉奔還，遵獨留不卻[19]。

translations.literal: When Gongsun Shu dispatched troops save Ao, Wu Han, Geng Yan etc all ran returned, Zun alone remained not withdrew [19].
translations.idiomatic: When Gongsun Shu sent troops to relieve Wei Ao, Wu Han and Geng Yan all fled back, but Zhai Zun alone did not retreat [19].

sentences.zh: 九年春，卒於軍。

translations.literal: Ninth year spring, died in army.
translations.idiomatic: He died in camp in the spring of the ninth year.

sentences.zh: 遵為人廉約小心，克己奉公，賞賜輒盡與士卒，家無私財，身衣韋稿，布被，夫人裳不加緣[20]，帝以是重焉。

translations.literal: Zun as man incorrupt restrained careful, conquered self served duke, rewards always all given soldiers, home no private wealth, body wore leather hemp, hemp quilt, wife's lower garments not added hem [20], emperor by this esteemed him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Zun was frugal and cautious, strict with himself and loyal to the public good: he gave every reward to the ranks, kept no private wealth, wore leather and hemp, slept under a hemp coverlet, and would not let his wife hem her skirts [20]—for this the emperor held him in the highest regard.

sentences.zh: 及卒，愍悼之尤甚。

translations.literal: When died, grieved mourned him especially deeply.
translations.idiomatic: When he died the ruler mourned him with unusual depth.

sentences.zh: 遵喪至河南縣，詔遣百官先會喪所，車駕素服臨之，望哭哀慟。

translations.literal: Zun's bier reached Henan county, edict dispatched hundred officials first assembled mourning place, imperial carriage plain clothes visited it, looked wept bitter grief.
translations.idiomatic: When the bier reached Henan county the edict had the high officials gather first at the mourning hall; the emperor came in plain dress, looked on the catafalque, and wept bitterly.

sentences.zh: 還幸城門，過其車騎，涕泣不能已[21]。

translations.literal: Returned visited city gate, passed his chariot horse, weeping tears could not stop [21].
translations.idiomatic: Returning, he stopped again at the city gate as Zhai Zun's escort passed and wept until he could not stop [21].

sentences.zh: 喪禮成，復親祠以太牢，如宣帝臨霍光故事[22]。

translations.literal: After mourning rites completed, again personally sacrificed with great victim, like Emperor Xuan visiting Huo Guang old precedent [22].
translations.idiomatic: When the funeral rites were done he sacrificed in person with a great victim, following the precedent of Emperor Xuan's visit to Huo Guang [22].

sentences.zh: 詔大長秋、謁者、河南尹護喪事，大司農給費。

translations.literal: Edict grand chief steward, herald, Henan governor supervised funeral affairs, grand minister of agriculture supplied expenses.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the grand chief steward, the herald, and the governor of Henan to supervise the funeral while the grand minister of agriculture paid the costs.

sentences.zh: 博士范升上疏，追稱遵曰：「臣聞先王崇政，尊美屏惡[23]。

translations.literal: Doctor Fan Sheng submitted memorial, pursued praised Zun saying: "Your subject hears former kings exalted government, honored beauty screened evil [23].
translations.idiomatic: Doctor Fan Sheng memorialized in praise of Zhai Zun: "I have heard that the kings of old exalted good government, honored the worthy, and barred the wicked [23].

sentences.zh: 昔高祖大聖，深見遠慮，班爵割地，與下分功，著錄勳臣，頌其德美。

translations.literal: Formerly Gaozu great sage, deep saw far thought, ranked nobles carved land, with subordinates divided merit, wrote record meritorious ministers, praised their virtue beauty.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu was a sage of deep foresight: he ranked nobles, carved out domains, shared merit with his helpers, recorded his meritorious ministers, and sang their virtues.

sentences.zh: 生則寵以殊禮，奏事不名，入門不趨[24]。

translations.literal: Alive then favored with special rites, memorial affairs not name, enter gate not hurry [24].
translations.idiomatic: In life they received extraordinary honors—memorials without naming them, entry to court without hurrying [24].

sentences.zh: 死則疇其爵邑，世無絕嗣[25]，丹書鐵券，傳於無窮[26]。

translations.literal: Dead then continued their noble fiefs, generations without cut-off heirs [25], vermilion writing iron tallies, transmitted to without end [26].
translations.idiomatic: In death their fiefs continued and their lines were never cut off [25]; vermilion writs and iron tallies passed down without end [26].

sentences.zh: 斯誠大漢厚下安人長久之德，所以累世十餘，歷載數百[27]，

translations.literal: This truly Great Han thick below settle people long-lasting's virtue, that whereby piled generations ten plus, passed reigns several hundred [27],
translations.idiomatic: Such was the Han's deep kindness to the people and its policy of endurance through many reigns and centuries [27],

sentences.zh: 廢而復興，絕而復續者也。

translations.literal: abolished yet again rose, cut off yet again continued.
translations.idiomatic: so that what fell was raised again and what seemed ended went on.

sentences.zh: 陛下以至德受命，先明漢道，□序輔佐，封賞功臣，同符祖宗。

translations.literal: Your Majesty with utmost virtue received mandate, first clarified Han way, set in order assisting hosts, enfeoffed rewarded meritorious ministers, matched tally ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty, receiving the mandate in the highest virtue, has clarified the Han way, set your helpers in order, and enfeoffed your meritorious servants in accord with the founders.

sentences.zh: 征虜將軍穎陽侯遵，不幸早薨。

translations.literal: Campaign Caitiffs general Yingyang marquis Zun, unluckily early died.
translations.idiomatic: The general who campaigns against the Caitiffs, Marquis Zun of Yingyang, has died before his time.

sentences.zh: 陛下仁恩，為之感傷，遠迎河南，惻怛之慟，形於聖躬，喪事用度，仰給縣官，重賜妻子，不可勝數。

translations.literal: Your Majesty benevolent grace, for him moved grieved, far welcomed Henan, sorrow's grief, formed on sagely person, funeral affairs expenses, upward supplied county office, heavy gifts wife children, cannot count.
translations.idiomatic: Your benevolence has moved you to grief, to go far to meet the bier in Henan and show sorrow in your own person; funeral costs drawn from the treasury and lavish gifts to his wife and children are beyond reckoning.

sentences.zh: 送死有以加生，厚亡有以過存，矯俗厲化，卓如日月[28]。

translations.literal: Sending death has thereby exceeded honoring life, thick mourning thereby passed caring for living, correcting customs fierce transformation, outstanding as sun moon [28].
translations.idiomatic: You have honored the dead beyond the living and mourned the lost more generously than the surviving—reforming custom in a splendor like sun and moon [28].

sentences.zh: 古者臣疾君視，臣卒君吊[29]，德之厚者也。

translations.literal: Ancient times subject sick ruler visited, subject died ruler condoled [29], virtue's thick ones.
translations.idiomatic: Of old the ruler visited a sick minister and condoled a dead one [29]—the mark of the deepest virtue.

sentences.zh: 陵□已來久矣。

translations.literal: Decline after Han rise since long already.
translations.idiomatic: That practice had long faded since the decline of the Later Han.

sentences.zh: 及至陛下，復興斯禮，腢下感動，莫不自勵。

translations.literal: Until Your Majesty, again raised this rite, subordinates moved, none not self-encouraged.
translations.idiomatic: Under Your Majesty it is revived; every officer is stirred to give his best.

sentences.zh: 臣竊見遵修行積善，竭忠於國，北平漁陽，西拒隴、蜀，先登坻上[30]，深取略陽。

translations.literal: Your subject humbly sees Zun cultivated conduct heaped good, exhausted loyalty to state, north pacified Yuyang, west barred Long, Shu, first ascended mound above [30], deeply took Lueyang.
translations.idiomatic: I have observed that Zhai Zun cultivated virtue and served the state with utter loyalty—pacifying Yuyang in the north, holding Long and Shu in the west, first scaling the Long heights [30], and boldly taking Lueyang.

sentences.zh: 觿兵既退，獨守磨難[31]。

translations.literal: Masses troops already retreated, alone guarded grinding hardship [31].
translations.idiomatic: When the army drew back he alone held on through the worst [31].

sentences.zh: 制御士心，不越法度。

translations.literal: Controlled regulated soldiers' hearts, not overstepped law degrees.
translations.idiomatic: He mastered his men's hearts without stepping outside the law.

sentences.zh: 所在吏人，不知有軍[32]。

translations.literal: Wherever located clerks people, not knew there was army [32].
translations.idiomatic: Wherever he was stationed the local people scarcely knew an army was camped among them [32].

sentences.zh: 清名聞於海內，廉白著於當世。

translations.literal: Clear name heard within seas, incorrupt white displayed on present age.
translations.idiomatic: His fair name filled the realm and his integrity shone in his own age.

sentences.zh: 所得賞賜，輒盡與吏士，身無奇衣，家無私財。

translations.literal: Obtained rewards, always all given clerks soldiers, body no strange clothes, home no private wealth.
translations.idiomatic: He passed every gift to officers and men, wore no finery, and kept no private fortune.

sentences.zh: 同產兄午以遵無子，娶妾送之，遵乃使人逆而不受，自以身任於國，不敢圖生慮繼嗣之計。

translations.literal: Full-birth elder brother Wu because Zun had no son, took concubine sent him, Zun then sent man decline not accept, self with body bore at state, not dare plan life consider posterity plan.
translations.idiomatic: His elder brother Wu, because Zhai Zun had no son, sent him a concubine; Zhai Zun sent her back, pledging his body to the state and refusing to think of heirs while alive.

sentences.zh: 臨死遺誡牛車載喪，薄葬洛陽。

translations.literal: Facing death left instructions ox cart carry bier, thin burial Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Dying, he ordered an ox cart for his bier and a plain burial in Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 問以家事，終無所言。

translations.literal: Asked about household affairs, to end said nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Asked about his household, he said nothing at the last.

sentences.zh: 任重道遠，死而後已[33]。

translations.literal: Burden heavy road long, die then only stopped [33].
translations.idiomatic: Heavy was the burden and long the road—he stopped only with death [33].

sentences.zh: 遵為將軍，取士皆用儒術，對酒設樂，必雅歌投壺[34]。

translations.literal: Zun as general, recruited scholars all used Confucian arts, facing wine set music, must refined song pitch-pot [34].
translations.idiomatic: As a general he chose his men for Confucian learning; at wine he set music and always sang the classical hymns and played pitch-pot [34].

sentences.zh: 又建為孔子立後，奏置五經大夫。

translations.literal: Also built for Confucius established heir, memorialized establish five classics grandees.
translations.idiomatic: He also had an heir appointed for Confucius and memorialized for doctors of the Five Classics.

translations.literal: 742 page.
translations.idiomatic: Source edition, page 742.

translations.literal: 744 page.
translations.idiomatic: Source edition, page 744.

sentences.zh: 無子，國除。

translations.literal: Had no son, state abolished.
translations.idiomatic: He had no son, and the marquisate was abolished.

sentences.zh: 兄午，官至酒泉太守。

translations.literal: Elder brother Wu, official rank reached Jiuquan governor.
translations.idiomatic: His elder brother Wu rose to be governor of Jiuquan.

translations.literal: Paternal cousin Rong [41].
translations.idiomatic: His paternal cousin was Zhai Rong [41].

sentences.zh: 肜字次孫，早孤，以至孝見稱。

translations.literal: Rong styled Cisun, early orphaned, by utmost filiality seen praised.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Rong, courtesy name Cisun, was orphaned young and was famed for his filial devotion.

sentences.zh: 遇天下亂，野無煙火，而獨在頤側。

translations.literal: Met world chaos, wilds no smoke fire, yet alone at widowed mother's side.
translations.idiomatic: When the realm fell into chaos and the countryside knew no hearthfires, he stayed alone at his widowed mother's side.

sentences.zh: 每賊過，見其尚幼而有志節，皆奇而哀之。

translations.literal: Whenever bandits passed, saw him still young yet had resolve integrity, all marveled pitied him.
translations.idiomatic: Raiders who passed saw that though he was a boy he had backbone, and they marveled and pitied him.

sentences.zh: 光武初以遵故，拜肜為黃門侍郎，常在左右。

translations.literal: Guangwu first because of Zun's sake, appointed Rong Yellow Gate gentleman-attendant, constantly at left right.
translations.idiomatic: At first Guangwu, for Zhai Zun's sake, named Zhai Rong a gentleman-attendant at the Yellow Gates and kept him always at his side.

sentences.zh: 及遵卒無子，帝追傷之，以肜為偃師長，令近遵墳墓，四時奉祠之。

translations.literal: When Zun died had no son, emperor pursued grieved him, made Rong Yanshi magistrate, ordered near Zun tomb, four seasons offer sacrifice it.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhai Zun died without an heir the emperor grieved and made Rong magistrate of Yanshi so he could tend the tomb and sacrifice at the four seasons.

sentences.zh: 肜有權略，視事五歲，縣無盜賊，課為第一，遷襄賁令[42]。

translations.literal: Rong had stratagem power, viewed affairs five years, county within no bandits, assessment was first, transferred Xiangben magistrate [42].
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Rong had resource and drive; in five years as magistrate he cleared the county of bandits, ranked first in assessment, and was moved to Xiangben [42].

sentences.zh: 時天下郡國尚未悉平，襄賁盜賊白日公行。

translations.literal: At that time under Heaven commanderies states not yet all pacified, Xiangben bandits daylight public walked.
translations.idiomatic: The empire was not yet fully pacified, and in Xiangben bandits walked abroad in broad daylight.

sentences.zh: 肜至，誅破奸猾，殄其支黨，數年，襄賁政清。

translations.literal: Rong arrived, executed broke crafty wicked, exterminated their branch faction, after several years, Xiangben government clear.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Rong arrived, executed the ringleaders, extirpated their gangs, and within a few years Xiangben was clean.

sentences.zh: 璽書勉勵，增秩一等，賜縑百匹。

translations.literal: Imperial edict encouraged exhorted, increased rank one grade, bestowed fine silk hundred bolts.
translations.idiomatic: An imperial rescript praised him, raised his rank one grade, and gave him a hundred bolts of silk.

sentences.zh: 當是時，匈奴、鮮卑及赤山烏桓連和強盛，數入塞殺略吏人。

translations.literal: At this time, Xiongnu, Xianbei and Chishan Wuhuan linked allied flourished strong, several times entered passes killed plundered clerks people.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu, Xianbei, and Chishan Wuhuan had allied and grown strong, raiding the passes again and again to kill and plunder.

sentences.zh: 朝廷以為憂，益增緣邊兵，郡有數千人，又遣諸將分屯障塞。

translations.literal: Court thought it worry, increased added along-border troops, commanderies had several thousand men, again dispatched various generals divide garrison barrier passes.
translations.idiomatic: The court was troubled, thickened the border garrisons to several thousand men per commandery, and sent generals to hold the defiles.

sentences.zh: 帝以肜為能，建武十七年，拜遼東太守。

translations.literal: Emperor because Rong capable, Jianwu seventeenth year, appointed Liaodong governor.
translations.idiomatic: Judging Zhai Rong able, in the seventeenth year of Jianwu the emperor named him governor of Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 至則勵兵馬，廣斥候。

translations.literal: Arrived then drilled horses and men, broadened scouts.
translations.idiomatic: On arrival he drilled horses and men and pushed out his scouts.

sentences.zh: 肜有勇力，能貫三百斤弓。

translations.literal: Rong had courage strength, could draw three hundred jin bow.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Rong was strong enough to draw a bow of three hundred jin.

sentences.zh: 虜每犯塞，常為士卒*[前]*鋒[43]，數破走之。

translations.literal: Caitiffs every violated border, always for soldiers front vanguard [43], several times defeated routed them.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever the raiders hit the frontier he always led from the front of his men [43] and repeatedly drove them off.

sentences.zh: 二十一年秋，鮮卑萬餘騎寇遼東，肜率數千人迎擊之，自被甲陷陳，虜大奔，投水死者過半，遂窮追出塞，虜急，皆棄兵祼身散走，斬首三千餘級，獲馬數千匹。

translations.literal: Twenty-first year autumn, Xianbei more than ten thousand horse raided Liaodong, Rong led several thousand men met struck them, self donned armor broke array, caitiffs great rout, threw water died more than half, thereupon exhaust pursued out barrier, caitiffs pressed, all abandoned weapons naked body scattered fled, beheaded heads more than three thousand grades, obtained horses several thousand.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the twenty-first year over ten thousand Xianbei horsemen raided Liaodong; Zhai Rong met them with a few thousand, fought in the van in full armor, and routed them so badly that more than half drowned fleeing across water.
